header.title: Live Location Sharing
header.abstract: This document defines a protocol for sharing continuously updating geographic location (live location) over XMPP. It specifies how a sender announces a timed sharing session, broadcasts position updates during the session, and terminates the session — either on expiry or by an explicit stop signal.

legal.copyright: This document is in the public domain.

section1.p: &xep0080; defines a way for XMPP entities to publish their geographic location at a point in time, either as a one-shot publication via &xep0163; (PEP) or as part of a message stanza. However, it does not define a mechanism for continuous, real-time location sharing within the context of an existing conversation — a common feature in modern messaging applications often called "live location" or "live location sharing".
This document defines a lightweight extension that allows one entity (the sender) to:

ol.li: announce a bounded live location session with an expiry time;
broadcast periodic position updates for the duration of the session;
terminate the session early via an explicit stop signal.

section1.p: Position payloads reuse the RFC 5870 geo: URI scheme, keeping the protocol simple and interoperable with existing location-aware clients.

ul.li: A sender MUST be able to start a live location session with a defined expiry time.
A sender MUST be able to send periodic position updates without creating new archived messages.
A sender MUST be able to stop sharing before the session expires.
A receiver MUST be able to determine the current position and session state from the conversation history alone (without retaining any server-side state).
Update stanzas MUST NOT be stored in the server-side message archive (&xep0313;).
The protocol MUST work in both one-to-one and multi-user (&xep0045;) conversations.

di.dd: A live location sharing episode with a defined start, an expiry time, and a unique session identifier.
The initial message stanza that starts a session. It is stored in the message archive and acts as the anchor for the session within conversation history.
A message stanza that carries a new geographic position for an active session. It is NOT stored in the message archive.
A message stanza that explicitly terminates a session before its expiry time. It is NOT stored in the message archive.
The entity that initiates a live location session and publishes position updates.
Any entity that receives stanzas belonging to the session.

section2.p: All elements defined in this document are qualified by the namespace urn:xmpp:live-location:0.

To begin sharing a live location, the sender transmits a normal message stanza whose body contains a geo: URI (RFC 5870) representing the initial position, and whose payload includes a <live-location> element carrying a unique session identifier and an expiry timestamp.
The <live-location> element has the following attributes:

di.dd: REQUIRED. A UUID (RFC 4122) that uniquely identifies this session. The same value MUST appear in all subsequent update and stop stanzas for this session.

REQUIRED. An &xep0082; DateTime string (e.g. 2026-05-19T14:30:00+02:00) indicating when the session automatically ends. After this time receivers MUST treat the session as inactive even if no explicit stop stanza was received.

section2.p: The session announcement message MUST be stored in the message archive so that receivers joining the conversation later can reconstruct session state from history.

example.caption: Sender starts a live location session

section2.example: <message from='juliet@capulet.lit/balcony' to='romeo@montague.lit' type='chat' id='msg-001'> <body>geo:48.858844,2.294351;u=10</body> <live-location xmlns='urn:xmpp:live-location:0' id='a4a5b7c2-1d3e-4f56-8901-abcdef012345' expires='2026-05-19T15:30:00+02:00'/> </message>
section2.p: The geo: URI body SHOULD include the u parameter (uncertainty in metres) when accuracy information is available, as defined in RFC 5870 §3.4.3.
Receiving clients SHOULD render the session announcement as an interactive map preview rather than displaying the raw URI text.
While the session is active, the sender transmits position updates as message stanzas with a <live-location-update> element. Each update stanza:

ul.li: MUST carry a geo: URI in the <body> reflecting the current position.
MUST include a <live-location-update> element with the id attribute matching the session identifier.
MUST include a <no-store> hint (&xep0334;) so that the stanza is not archived. Update stanzas carry transient data; only the session announcement is the durable record.
SHOULD NOT be sent after the session expires timestamp has passed.

section2.p: The recommended update interval is implementation-defined. Implementations SHOULD respect battery and bandwidth constraints and SHOULD use a minimum interval of 5 seconds.
A sender MAY terminate a session before its expires time by sending a message stanza containing a <live-location-stop> element with the session id. This stanza:

ul.li: MUST NOT carry a message body.

section2.p: Upon receiving a stop stanza, receivers MUST immediately treat the referenced session as inactive, regardless of whether the expires timestamp has passed.

A sender whose client restarts while a session is still within its expiry window MUST send a stop stanza upon reconnecting, since it is no longer able to provide updates for that session. This ensures receivers are promptly informed even if no formal stop was sent before the restart.
A live location session transitions through the following states:

ol.li: Active — after the session announcement is sent and before either the expires time is reached or a stop stanza is received.
Expired — the expires timestamp has passed. No stop stanza was received.

Stopped — a <live-location-stop> stanza was received for this session ID. An explicit stop takes precedence over the expiry timestamp: a session that was stopped MUST be treated as inactive even if expires is still in the future.

section2.p: Receivers that only have access to the archived session announcement (e.g. after rejoining a chat) MUST compare the current time against expires to determine whether the session is still potentially active. They SHOULD treat it as active and display an interactive map if the current time is before expires AND no stop stanza has been received. The most recently received position from either the announcement or any update stanzas SHOULD be used as the displayed location.

The protocol operates identically in &xep0045; rooms. The sender addresses stanzas to the bare JID of the room. All occupants receive announcement, update, and stop stanzas via the normal room distribution mechanism.

Because update and stop stanzas carry <no-store>, they are not archived by the room's MAM service. A receiver that connects after a session has started will see only the session announcement in the archive and MUST infer session state from its expires attribute and the presence or absence of subsequent archived stop stanzas (if any).

Note: stop stanzas are not archived. A receiver joining after a sender stopped will see the announcement and infer the session is active if expires is in the future. This is a known limitation; implementations may choose to send an archived stop message at their discretion, trading archive cleanliness for correctness.

ol.li: A sender MUST NOT start more than one active live location session per conversation at a time. If the sender wishes to begin a new session, it MUST first stop the existing one.
Receivers MUST silently ignore update and stop stanzas whose id does not match any known active session.
Receivers MUST ignore update stanzas received after the session's expires time.
The session announcement MUST be treated as the first position data point; receivers MUST use it as the initial map position before any update stanza arrives.
A receiver MUST use the position from the most recently received update stanza (or the announcement if no updates have arrived) as the current displayed location.
Implementations SHOULD throttle map preview refreshes (e.g. no more frequently than once per minute) to avoid overloading tile servers or consuming excessive bandwidth.

section1.p: If an entity supports live location sharing as defined in this document, it MUST advertise that fact by including the following feature in its service discovery information (&xep0030;) responses:

&xep0080; (User Location) defines a PEP-based mechanism for publishing a user's current location to subscribers. It uses a rich XML element with typed sub-elements for latitude, longitude, altitude, accuracy, and many semantic fields (country, region, street, etc.).
This document takes a different approach, optimised for the conversation context:

tr.td: RFC 5870 geo: URI in message body

section1.p: Implementations SHOULD use XEP-0080 for ambient "what is my current location" publishing and this protocol for the distinct use case of intentional, conversation-scoped, time-bounded location sharing.
Live location sharing exposes precise physical location data in real time. Implementations MUST therefore:

ul.li: Require explicit, informed user consent before starting a session. The UI MUST make the session duration clearly visible before the user confirms.

Display an unambiguous, persistent indicator (e.g. a status bar notification) on the sender's device for the entire duration of an active session, including a one-tap action to stop sharing immediately.
Automatically stop all active sessions if the application is restarted or the account disconnects, and transmit the corresponding stop stanza upon reconnection.

Only offer this feature over encrypted transports. Sessions SHOULD only be started when the conversation uses transport-layer encryption (TLS). The feature SHOULD be disabled when end-to-end encryption (e.g. OMEMO, OpenPGP) is active, as the geo: URI body is not encrypted by those mechanisms and would be transmitted in cleartext to the server.
Clearly communicate to the user which parties will receive their location (all participants of the conversation, including any server-side logging).

section1.p: Receivers MUST NOT forward or re-publish position data received from a live location session without the explicit consent of the sender.
Servers MUST NOT store update or stop stanzas in the message archive (the <no-store> hint serves this purpose), but clients SHOULD NOT rely solely on server-side enforcement of this hint.
This document requires no interaction with &IANA;.

section2.p: The ®ISTRAR; shall include urn:xmpp:live-location:0 in its registry of protocol namespaces.

section1.code:
<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?>
<xs:schema xmlns:xs='http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema' targetNamespace='urn:xmpp:live-location:0' xmlns='urn:xmpp:live-location:0' elementFormDefault='qualified'>
<!-- Session announcement: child of <message> --> <xs:element name='live-location'> <xs:complexType> <xs:attribute name='id' type='xs:string' use='required'/> <xs:attribute name='expires' type='xs:dateTime' use='required'/> </xs:complexType> </xs:element>
<!-- Position update: child of <message> --> <xs:element name='live-location-update'> <xs:complexType> <xs:attribute name='id' type='xs:string' use='required'/> </xs:complexType> </xs:element>
<!-- Explicit session stop: child of <message> --> <xs:element name='live-location-stop'> <xs:complexType> <xs:attribute name='id' type='xs:string' use='required'/> </xs:complexType> </xs:element>
</xs:schema>
